text.title: rust-testing-ci-toolchain Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for rust-testing-ci-toolchain. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use cargo nextest run in CI instead of cargo test. Configure .config/nextest.toml with fail-fast = false, retries = 1 on the ci profile, and a slow-timeout to catch hangs. Never use cargo test in the main CI job for a project that has integration tests.
rule.rationale: cargo test runs tests sequentially within a binary and has no per-test timeout. A single hanging test blocks the entire suite silently. nextest runs each test in its own process with a configurable timeout, emits JUnit XML, and parallelizes across all available cores.

rule.statement: Upload cargo llvm-cov --lcov output to Codecov or Coveralls on every PR. Do not enforce a coverage percentage gate in CI — coverage is a signal for reviewers, not a binary pass/fail. A drop in coverage on a PR is a conversation item, not a block.
rule.rationale: Hard coverage gates incentivize writing trivial tests that inflate coverage without testing behavior. Soft signals (uploaded diff coverage) keep reviewers informed without gamification.

rule.statement: Run cargo +nightly miri test on every PR for crates that contain unsafe blocks or FFI. Gate miri on its own CI job (it is slow); do not block the main test job on it.
rule.rationale: Miri catches undefined behavior — wrong pointer offsets, uninitialized reads, data races in unsafe Send/Sync — that the Rust compiler cannot detect at compile time. These bugs are silent in release builds and manifest as memory corruption in production.

rule.statement: Pin cargo nextest, cargo llvm-cov, and cargo miri versions in CI using taiki-e/install-action and manage updates via Renovate. Never use @latest or a floating version in CI tool installs.
rule.rationale: Floating @latest versions can break CI silently when a new release changes behavior or output format. Pinned versions + Renovate gives reproducible CI with automated upgrade PRs.

rule.statement: Configure a CI build cache for ~/.cargo/registry, ~/.cargo/git, and target/ keyed on the rust toolchain version and Cargo.lock hash. Rebuild on Cargo.lock change; otherwise restore from cache. This applies to both cargo test and cargo nextest runs.
rule.rationale: Slow link times inflate CI minutes. On a medium Rust service, a cold build of test binaries can take 3-5 minutes. With a warm cache, incremental rebuilds take 20-30 seconds.
